entities.name: HEADCAGE TECHNIQUE
entities.description: A standardized technique used to screen sorghum for resistance to the mirid head bug

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop being screened for resistance to the mirid head bug

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum to withstand infestation by the mirid head bug

entities.name: MIRID HEAD BUG
entities.description: Eurystylus immaculatus Odh., a pest that infests sorghum panicles

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The region where the headcage technique was standardized

entities.name: HALF-ANTHESIS TO COMPLETE-ANTHESIS STAGE
entities.description: The growth stage of panicles when infestation leads to maximum population build-up and grain damage

entities.name: POPULATION BUILD-UP
entities.description: The increase in the number of mirid head bugs on the panicles

entities.name: GRAIN DAMAGE
entities.description: The physical harm caused to sorghum grains by the mirid head bug

entities.name: LOSS IN GRAIN MASS
entities.description: Reduction in the weight of grains due to infestation

entities.name: GRAIN HARDNESS
entities.description: The physical hardness of the grains, affected by infestation

entities.name: SEED GERMINATION
entities.description: The ability of seeds to sprout, which is reduced by infestation

entities.name: GRAIN MOULD INCIDENCE
entities.description: The occurrence of mould on grains, which increases with higher grain damage

entities.name: PANICLE SIZE
entities.description: The size of the sorghum panicle, which influences bug numbers and damage levels
